# Sales Data Analysis using Spark

## Objective
Analyze sales trends

### Steps
- Load data from ADLS
- Transform using Spark
- Generate KPIs

### Code

```python
df = spark.read.csv("abfss://container/file.csv")
print("Hello") 
print("World")
```

#Read from Data Lake
df = spark.read.format("csv").option("inferschema",True).load("abfss://container1@we47storage1.dfs.core.windows.net/sourcedir/txns") \
.toDF("txnid","txndate","custid","amount","category","product","city","state","paytype")
df.printSchema()
df.show(2)

# Save as a standard Spark managed table
df.write.mode("overwrite").saveAsTable("default.txnstbl")

# Alternatively, save as a specific format (like Parquet or Delta)
#df.write.format("parquet").mode("append").saveAsTable("default.txnstbl")

from pyspark.sql.functions import count,sum,round
df1 = df.groupBy("state").agg(count("txnid").alias("TotalTrans"),round(sum("amount"),2).alias("TotalSales"))
df1.show()

#Write into ADLS
df1.write.format("parquet").save("abfss://container1@we47storage1.dfs.core.windows.net/outputdir/")
print("Data Written into Data Lake")

#Read from data lake(External Storage) using Managed Identity

#Provide Storage blob data contributor access to the synapse workspace idenitity

# Ensure the path matches the account name above
path = "abfss://container1@we47storage1.dfs.core.windows.net/sourcedir/txns"

df = spark.read.format("csv") \
    .option("header", True) \
    .option("inferSchema", True) \
    .load(path)
df.show()

#List files
from notebookutils import mssparkutils
lst = mssparkutils.fs.ls("abfss://container1@we47storage1.dfs.core.windows.net")

# Define your destination table path: <DatabaseName>.<SchemaName>.<TableName>
target_table = "dedsqlpool.dbo.tbl1"

# Write the DataFrame using the Synapse SQL connector
df.write \
    .format("com.microsoft.spark.sqlanalytics") \
    .option("Constants.SERVER_NAME", "izsynapseworkspace.sql.azuresynapse.net") \
    .option("Constants.USER_NAME", "izadmin") \
    .option("Constants.PASSWORD", "Inceptez@123") \
    .option("Constants.DATA_BASE", "dedsqlpool") \
    .mode("overwrite") \
    .synapsesql(target_table)

# 1. Define the table reference
# Use the format: <DatabaseName>.<SchemaName>.<TableName>
dedicated_table = "delsqlpool.dbo.tbl1"

# 2. Read the dedicated table into a Spark DataFrame
df = spark.read.synapsesql(dedicated_table)

# Show data
display(df.limit(10))
